primary_purpose: afi-labs is the AFI org's explicitly-experimental private R&D sandbox ("junk drawer for experiments") holding prototypes, PoCs, stub scaffolds, and design-note drafts that are meant to graduate into production repos only after proving stable; it ships no normative protocol artifacts and no real implementation.
classification_rationale: Self-described as experimental: README.md:4-5 "AFI Labs is the experimental playground... Anything that isn't production-ready - prototypes, PoCs, research notebooks, speculative integrations - lives here first." .afi-codex.json:3-4 sets role "Experimental playground for prototypes and PoCs" and status "experimental"; .afi-codex.json:14 "droid_ready": false; provides only experimental-prototypes/proof-of-concepts/research-experiments with empty dependsOn/consumers. README.md:22 graduation rule: modules promote to afi-core/afi-agents once stable. No package.json anywhere; doc spec files ARCHITECTURE.md/OBSERVABILITY.md/PIPELINE_FORMAT.md/PLUGIN_SPEC.md are 0 bytes; all 11 whitepaper section stubs are 2-line placeholders; Solidity contracts and the replay engine are literal "// Placeholder" stubs. This is RESEARCH, not NORMATIVE/REFERENCE_IMPL: nothing here is a binding interoperability rule or a working reference spine.
protocol_touchpoints: schemas (agent registry pydantic + agent_spec template; no canonical AFI schemas)
replay/determinism (empty placeholder engine + codex replay_endpoint string)
registries/reputation (agent registry servers, ghost/omnichain registry configs)

reference_only_assumptions.quote: # Vercel + MongoDB MVP Starter (AFI Protocol) ... /lib/tssd.ts — Logic to write to timeseries collection ... db.createCollection("tssd", { timeseries: ... })
## Build Now - Signal submission (API) - MongoDB T.S.S.D. write with timeseries schema
[ ] Store signal in MongoDB (timeseries with metadata) ... [ ] Create timeseries collections in MongoDB for T.S.S.D.
MONGO_URI = os.getenv("MONGO_URI", "mongodb://localhost:27017") ... app = FastAPI(title="AFI Agent Registry API with MongoDB")
[ ] Fork or clone ElizaOS as signal processing framework base ... [ ] Trigger scoring model (static logic or Eliza integration)

contradictions.title: TSSD modeled as MongoDB-only timeseries store (collapses evidence plane onto one engine)
contradictions.detail: The MVP scaffold and roadmap define T.S.S.D. exclusively as a MongoDB Atlas timeseries collection with a flat metadata schema, contradicting the portable-protocol rule that the evidence/vault plane is multi-engine (Mongo/PostgreSQL/TimescaleDB/InfluxDB per vault.schema.json) and must conform to the canonical vault schema. This is the exact 'Mongo tunnel vision' tension. Mitigated by RESEARCH status (pre-spec MVP doc, no production path).
contradictions.evidence: docs/specs/vercel_mongo_scaffold.md:14-23 'MongoDB Timeseries Setup: db.createCollection("tssd", { timeseries: { timeField: "timestamp" ...}})'; docs/plans/afi_mvp_what_to_stub_vs_build.md:6 'MongoDB T.S.S.D. write with timeseries schema'

contradictions.title: No canonical USS/CPJ/vault/pipeline schemas; signal schema reduced to ad-hoc Mongo metadata
contradictions.detail: USS appears only as the title of an empty 2-line stub (section-4) and the 'Universal Signal Schema' is described as a single sentence; the only concrete signal shape is the loose Mongo metadata blob in the scaffold. A research sandbox carrying a divergent informal signal shape risks being mistaken for canon if promoted without conforming to afi-config schemas.
contradictions.evidence: docs/sections/section-4-universal-signal-schema.md:1-2 'Section 4: Universal Signal Schema / Formal JSON structure of all signals.'; docs/specs/vercel_mongo_scaffold.md:25-37 'Suggested Metadata Schema: { timestamp, metadata: { agentId, source, enrichment, score, uuid }}'

contradictions.title: Replay/determinism advertised as capability but engine is an empty placeholder
contradictions.detail: Codex configs declare replay endpoints and a 'replay' capability and the CLI spec claims 'deterministic, auditable' tone, yet pipelines/replay/replay_engine.py and replay_runner.py are literal placeholder comments and the toy pipeline scorer uses Math.random() (non-deterministic). No replay-critical or determinism artifacts exist here. Info-level since the repo is explicitly experimental.
contradictions.evidence: pipelines/replay/replay_engine.py:1 '# Placeholder content for replay_engine.py'; code-sandbox/core/pipeline-runner.ts:26 'return { ...input, score: Math.random().toFixed(2) };'; config/codex/afi-codex.json:8,13-17 '"replay_endpoint": ... "capabilities": ["scoring","replay","validation"]'

contradictions.title: Mint/on-chain commitment exists only as mock/placeholder, no receipt or emissions types
contradictions.detail: $AFI minting is explicitly stubbed as a console-log/mock; on-chain deploy scripts (Base, Solana) and Solidity contracts (OFTv2DualMint.sol, ScarlettGenesisNFT.sol) are empty placeholders. No AFIMintCoordinator/AFISignalReceipt/EmissionsMinted/coordinateMint/beneficiary/epoch symbols are present. The commitment plane is absent here, consistent with RESEARCH classification.
contradictions.evidence: docs/plans/afi_mvp_what_to_stub_vs_build.md:13 '$AFI minting logic -> use mocked console log or token event queue'; misc/contracts/OFTv2DualMint.sol:1 '// Placeholder for contracts/OFTv2DualMint.sol'; misc/deploy/deploy_to_base.js:1 '// Placeholder for scripts/deploy_to_base.js'

notable_findings.title: Repo is explicitly an experimental/junk-drawer R&D sandbox, not protocol law
notable_findings.evidence: README.md:4-5 'AFI Labs is the experimental playground for Agentic Financial Intelligence. Anything that isn't production-ready - prototypes, PoCs, research notebooks, speculative integrations - lives here first.'; .afi-codex.json:3-4 role 'Experimental playground for prototypes and PoCs', status 'experimental'
notable_findings.recommendation: Treat as RESEARCH; exclude from normative synthesis. Ensure any downstream graduation re-validates against afi-config canonical schemas before promotion to afi-core/afi-agents.

notable_findings.title: MongoDB tunnel-vision: TSSD defined as a single Mongo timeseries collection
notable_findings.evidence: docs/specs/vercel_mongo_scaffold.md:11 '/lib/tssd.ts — Logic to write to timeseries collection'; :16-23 db.createCollection("tssd", { timeseries: {...} }); docs/plans/afi_mvp_roadmap.md:14 '[ ] Store signal in MongoDB (timeseries with metadata)'
notable_findings.recommendation: Flag as pre-portable-protocol MVP doc. If any of this graduates, replace single-engine TSSD with the multi-engine vault per vault.schema.json (Mongo/PostgreSQL/TimescaleDB/InfluxDB).

notable_findings.title: USS/CPJ and whitepaper sections are empty 2-line stubs; no canonical schema present
notable_findings.evidence: docs/sections/section-4-universal-signal-schema.md:1-2 'Section 4: Universal Signal Schema / Formal JSON structure of all signals.' (whole file); section-5-tssd-storage.md and section-2-signal-lifecycle.md likewise 2-line stubs
notable_findings.recommendation: No action beyond noting; canonical USS v1.1 lives in afi-config, not here. Do not source signal-schema canon from this repo.

notable_findings.title: Replay engine + on-chain deploy + Solidity contracts are literal placeholder stubs
notable_findings.evidence: pipelines/replay/replay_engine.py:1 '# Placeholder content for replay_engine.py'; pipelines/replay/replay_runner.py:1 same; misc/contracts/OFTv2DualMint.sol:1 '// Placeholder ...'; code-sandbox/infra/deploy_to_solana.ts:1 '// Placeholder ...'
notable_findings.recommendation: None; confirms zero replay-critical/commitment artifacts in this repo.

notable_findings.title: Toy pipeline-runner uses non-deterministic Math.random() scorer
notable_findings.recommendation: None; experimental spike. Note it violates protocol determinism but is not on any production path.

notable_findings.title: Agent registry assumes Mongo backend and a 'access_tssd' permission model
notable_findings.evidence: code-sandbox/agents/registry/agent_registry_server_mongo.py:15 FastAPI title '...with MongoDB'; :37 permissions example '["score_signals", "access_tssd"]'; :11 default MONGO_URI localhost:27017
notable_findings.recommendation: Reference-only registry prototype; the canonical registry/reputation spec is REGISTRIES_AND_REPUTATION.v0.1.md in afi-docs. Reputation here must remain selection/allocation-only, not override scoring.

notable_findings.title: Codex configs reference live afi.node.com replay/log endpoints and DID/validator identity (aspirational)
notable_findings.evidence: config/codex/afi-codex.json:6-18 'node_type: validator, did: did:ethr:..., log_endpoint/replay_endpoint https://afi.node.com/api/..., capabilities [scoring, replay, validation], schema_version 0.1.0'
notable_findings.recommendation: None; sample/template identity doc, not a normative manifest. Do not treat as plugin-manifest canon.

notable_findings.title: No BigQuery/Kafka/warehouse/Postgres/Timescale/Influx references; analytics & multi-engine vault entirely absent
notable_findings.evidence: grep across repo for BigQuery|kafka|warehouse|postgres|timescaledb|influxdb|pubsub returned zero hits (only MongoDB appears as a storage engine)
notable_findings.recommendation: Confirms the single-engine (Mongo) framing; analytics plane not modeled here. Consistent with RESEARCH status.

notable_findings.title: No package.json / dependency manifest anywhere; dependency graph is documentation-only
notable_findings.evidence: `find . -name package.json` returns nothing; .afi-codex.json:12-13 dependsOn [] and consumers []
notable_findings.recommendation: Dependencies (ElizaOS, MongoDB, Vercel, LayerZero) are aspirational from roadmap docs, not wired code. Record as upstream intent only.
